summary: Excellent soundboard‑matrix recording of a rain‑soaked RFK show with a monster Victim > Foolish > Dark Star and stellar Jerry, Brent and Phil performances.
review: The crowd‑filled RFK concert is remembered as one of the most iconic summer‑’90 shows – a torrential storm turned into extra energy, a first set that kept the party rolling and a second set highlighted by an epic Victim > Foolish > Dark Star sequence, Box of Rain and a soaring The Weight.  The source is a soundboard capture with just enough audience bleed to convey the atmosphere; most listeners describe it as crystal‑clear and “excellent,” despite one complaint about clipping.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 80 reviews, >90% five‑star, uniformly praising the performance and recording; only a single low‑rating comment about clipping.  Show quality is top‑tier (70 % weight) and recording quality is excellent (30 % weight), yielding an overall rating above 4.5.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, well‑balanced mix with just enough crowd bleed to feel the rain‑storm ambience; a few listeners note occasional clipping or slight matrix‑style audience chatter but overall no drop‑outs.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Rain opened the first set, fueling a high‑octane start; after a brief rain pause the second set exploded with Victim > Foolish → Dark Star → Drums/Space, then a heartfelt Hey Jude reprise and a crowd‑pleasing Touch of Grey encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for strong vocals, soaring guitar leads and an especially intense solo during Dark Star.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for “tremendous” bass work and vocal moments; several reviewers singled out his interplay on the second set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboardist) receives glowing remarks – his final Dark Star, vocal on Mr. Fantasy and overall keyboard texture are highlighted as standout.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey’s drum sound gets mixed feedback; a few mention “drum mix” issues while others celebrate the powerful Drums/Space section.
